description: To restore a damaged or worn thing to working condition through diagnosis and technical work. The general verb 'whori' (mend) covers mending; 'shiroka' centres on recovering function, especially in a device or system.
articulatory_notes: With 'shi', the blade of the tongue approaches the postalveolar region and lets air pass into high 'i'. Stress falls on trilled 'ro'; final 'ka' closes at the soft palate before releasing into open 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The word travels from sustained friction through a trilled centre to the clean release of 'ka'. That movement can recall technical work carried through to a definite finish.
usage_notes: The verb is transitive, and its event noun is the resulting repair. Its objects are commonly devices or larger structures and systems whose function can be restored.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Technical repair keeps a device, its materials, and the work already in it available for further use.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A tool or household object once had to be understood well enough to fix. The verb 'shiroka' names that skilled return to function.

semantic_domains.creation: technical restoration of a damaged object's function
semantic_domains.wisdom: the diagnosis and skilled work needed to make a device usable again
